thesis: NVIDIA is the dominant designer of GPUs and AI accelerators, and the foundational enabler of the generative AI revolution. FY2025 revenue of $130.5B (+114% YoY) and Q1 FY2027 revenue of $81.6B (+85% YoY) demonstrate sustained hypergrowth. The CUDA software ecosystem creates deep developer lock-in, while the Blackwell architecture ramp and agentic AI positioning extend the moat. At ~$5T market cap and ~35x TTM P/E, valuation is elevated but justified by 75% gross margins, $60B+ annual free cash flow, and a structurally expanding AI TAM.

drivers: Blackwell demand exceeds supply through 2027; agentic AI and sovereign AI drive new TAM
Gross margins expand to 78% on software mix and pricing power
Stock re-rates to 35x forward earnings on sustained 50%+ growth
Automotive and robotics become meaningful revenue contributors by 2028

risks: AI capex cycle peaks in 2026; hyperscaler demand decelerates sharply
Competition from AMD MI350X, custom ASICs (AVGO, MRVL), and Cerebras gains share
Margins compress to 65% on mix shift or pricing pressure
Geopolitical export controls expand beyond China to constrain TAM
Multiple contracts to 20x on cyclical normalization

base_case.assumptions: Data center growth moderates to 50–60% YoY; gaming and automotive contribute modestly; margins stable at 75%; stock trades at 28–30x forward earnings. FY2027 revenue of $240–260B with EPS of $7.50–8.50.

catalysts.event: Q2 FY2027 earnings — Blackwell ramp and guidance critical
NVIDIA GTC 2026 — new product announcements and roadmap updates
Q3 FY2027 earnings — holiday gaming season + data center update
CES 2027 — automotive and consumer AI announcements

key_risks: Customer concentration: hyperscalers (Microsoft, Meta, Google, Amazon, Oracle) drive majority of data center revenue
Competition from AMD, custom ASICs (Broadcom, Marvell), and Cerebras wafer-scale chips
Valuation risk at ~$5T market cap and ~35x TTM P/E; law of large numbers makes sustained hypergrowth harder
Geopolitical export controls on advanced AI chips could expand beyond China
TSMC supply chain dependency; any disruption to Taiwan production would halt NVIDIA manufacturing
AI capex cycle maturation; if AI ROI disappoints, hyperscaler spending could slow abruptly
